name: Sushi
recipeIngredient: 3 1/3 cups short grain white rice (Nishiki is best, Kohkuo Rose is mediocre, Tamaki is good, Botan Calrose is bad)
recipeInstructions:
1. Buy a sushi mat and bamboo sticks to make a square floppy mat.
2. Wash the rice until water runs clear and let it stand for a few minutes.
3. Place the rice in a pot with 4 cups of water and kombu, bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat, cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Remove the lid and place an absorbent kitchen towel over the top of the pot, then replace the lid and turn off the heat. Let the rice stand undisturbed for 15 minutes.
5. If there is still water on top of the rice after 5 minutes of simmering, allow it to simmer longer before turning off the heat.
6. Dissolve 5 tablespoons white sugar in 5 tablespoons rice vinegar and add 4 teaspoons sea salt. Adjust seasoning as desired.
7. Turn the rice out onto a shallow bowl with slightly porous material (wood is recommended) and gently stir with a flat spoon, keeping the rice grains intact.
8. Sprinkle the seasoning mixture onto the rice as you stir until it becomes slightly shiny and more sticky.
9. Lay a sheet of nori on the sushi mat with the long edge parallel to the bottom.
10. Spread a thin layer (1/2 inch) of rice evenly on the nori, covering all but half an inch at the bottom and one and a half inches at the top.
11. Place your desired fillings in the center of the rice.
12. Roll up the nori around the rice and filling, bringing the bottom edge of rice and nori over the filling to meet the top edge of the rice layer and seal the roll.
13. Wrap the sushi mat around the roll to gently compress it while maintaining its cylindrical shape.
14. Slice the roll with a very sharp knife.
15. Prepare additional fillings by cutting them into approximately 1/4 inch strips. Some items, such as shiitake mushrooms and tofu, may need to be simmered in seasoning broth made from rice wine, water, sugar, and soy sauce.
16. Simmer vegetables like carrots, mushrooms, tempeh, pickled plums, fresh tofu, baked marinated tofu, and kampyo gourds separately in a separate broth until desired flavor is achieved.
17. Slice the simmered items into strips before adding to the sushi roll.
18. Add avocado for a "feast" style sushi roll.
19. Sprinkle sesame seeds on top for added crunch if desired.
20. Remove any fillings that taste bad, such as sweet red pepper, from the sushi rolls.
